name: Aisha
observedShift: Aisha is in week four, remote, and has started driving planning threads, but she still looks uncertain in cross-functional meetings when priorities conflict. I need to understand what is still unclear around decision rights, partner expectations, and how to run alignment without over-indexing on consensus. She has good energy, but I do not want her spending it decoding unspoken norms. This meeting should surface the specific friction points and translate them into practical support and clearer boundaries.

focusPoints.label: Decision rights and what you own end-to-end.
focusPoints.reason: Whether the scope of her planning threads and decision authority is still fuzzy—especially when priorities conflict across teams—so you can clarify what “good” looks like for her role.

focusPoints.label: Partner expectations when priorities conflict.
focusPoints.reason: How she’s handling partner pushback and competing asks in cross-functional meetings—whether she’s getting clear expectations up front or still guessing what others need from her.

focusPoints.label: Running alignment without over-indexing consensus.
focusPoints.reason: What she’s using today to align teams (updates, drafts, decision memos, escalation paths) and where it’s causing extra cycles—so you can help her choose a lighter-weight alignment pattern that still lands decisions.

focusPoints.label: What support and boundaries you’ll provide.
focusPoints.reason: What she would want more of from you to reduce decoding unspoken norms—e.g., explicit escalation rules, example decisions, and a clear “when to decide vs when to align” boundary for her first quarter.
